commit.message:
feat: initialize shoal monorepo scaffold

Bazel 9 + bzlmod workspace with hexagonal architecture:
- domain/ — pure business logic interfaces
- adapter/ — external implementations (DHT, BT, caterwaul, NATS, etc.)
- proto/ — buf-managed protobuf (events + search service)
- cmd/ — six service binaries

Event bus: NATS JetStream. Search: caterwaul (Pebble + roaring BM25).
Proto contracts for InfoHashDiscovered → MetadataRetrieved pipeline.

commit.message:
refactor: remove dead code and consolidate peer state (#100)

* refactor: remove dead code and consolidate peer state

- Remove ClockSync offset computation (dead code — never applied anywhere;
  ClockSync now tracks RTT only via median over a sliding window of 8 samples)
- Remove IntervalPlayer (superseded by IntervalRing)
- Extract PeerRegistry and IpcWriterPool into wail-tauri/peers.rs,
  replacing 11 scattered HashMaps with a single typed registry
- Add remove_peer_fully() helper eliminating duplicated cleanup between
  PeerLeft and PeerFailed-give-up paths
- Update CLAUDE.md, docs/networking.md, docs/architecture.md,
  docs/test-strategies.md, docs/missing-tests-punchlist.md, tradeoffs.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: fix stale variable references in networking.md

Update §7, §8, §9 to reflect the PeerRegistry refactor:
- hello_sent HashSet → PeerRegistry::mark/clear_hello_sent()
- slot_affinity/slot_occupied/peer_slots → SlotAllocator/PeerState
- peer_reconnect_attempts HashMap → PeerState::reconnect_attempts
- signaling reconnect "on success" list → PeerRegistry::reset_for_reconnect()
- wire format version 1 → 2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: extend signaling server infra and add integration tests

- Add TestServerConfig to the in-process signaling server with support
  for min_version enforcement (426), room passwords (401), room capacity
  limits (409), and rate-limit mode (429)
- Add semver_cmp helper and semver_comparison_basic unit test
- Add signaling_tests.rs with 11 §1.x and §6.3 integration tests:
  client_version_too_old, correct_client_version_accepted,
  wrong_password_rejected, correct_password_accepted,
  room_at_capacity_rejected, display_name_in_peer_joined,
  rate_limit_returns_429, eviction_triggers_session_reconnect,
  room_deleted_when_last_peer_leaves, duplicate_message_not_redelivered,
  stale_peer_cleaned_after_timeout
- Add network.rs integration tests: three_peers_exchange_audio,
  one_peer_leaves_three_peer_room_others_continue,
  higher_id_re_initiate_does_not_create_offer,
  duplicate_peer_failed_signals_deduplicated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add changeset for peer registry refactor

* feat: display app version in UI header

Show the running app version (via Tauri's app.getVersion()) in the top-right
of the join screen. Also adds the required core:app:default capability and
removes a stale CLAUDE.md reference to a removed crossfade param.

commit.message:
Fix AI extractor misclassifying completion payment as installment

- Edge function: detect completion payments hiding in milestones by label
  ("completion", "handover", "balance") or construction at 100%, move to
  onHandoverPercent
- Gemini prompt: add explicit rule #6 with example showing that "Payment
  On Completion" goes in onHandoverPercent, never in milestones[]
- Client-side safety net: applyExtractedPlan.ts detects and moves
  completion payments if edge function missed them

Fixes the Frank Muller 3011 case where 70% completion was treated as
a regular installment because the document lists it in the same section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: replace planMode boolean with StreamMode registry (#397)

* refactor: replace planMode boolean with StreamMode registry

Replace the binary isPlanMode flag with a StreamMode enum ("default", "plan",
"superadmin") and a centralized STREAM_MODES registry that defines per-mode
tool allowlists, MCP enablement, and SDK permission modes. This eliminates
scattered planMode checks and makes adding new modes trivial.

- Delete planModeStore.ts, add streamModeStore.ts
- Add STREAM_MODES config with sdkTools allowlists and mcpEnabled flags
- Update stream route, worker-entry, agent-constants, and sdk-tools-sync
- Update all tests and type guards for new StreamMode type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pass streamMode explicitly through agent pipeline

- Pass streamMode from stream route through agent-child-runner to run-agent
- Use explicit streamMode in run-agent instead of deriving from permissionMode
- Use STREAM_MODES registry for MCP enablement and permission mode defaults
- Validate streamMode in both run-agent and worker-entry with fallback logging
- Disable MCP servers and MCP extraTools when mode config says mcpEnabled=false

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update worker-pool tests and import-order checks for refactored code

- Fix import-order test regexes to handle reformatted multi-line imports
- Update owner-key-contract test to point at attempts.ts instead of executor.ts
- Strip comments before checking required imports in check-import-order script
- Pass TEST_WORKER_MODE env in test builds via env-isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review comments

- Remove `as` type assertions and `!` non-null assertions in stream-tools.ts
  using `.some()` pattern for type-safe value comparison
- Extract narrowed `modeTools` variable to satisfy tsgo closure narrowing
- Guard E2B sandbox setup when stream mode has mcpEnabled=false

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add stream mode selector for superadmins

- Non-superadmins: same plan mode toggle as before (simple icon button)
- Superadmins: dropdown with Default/Plan/Terminal modes
- Terminal mode (superadmin): bash-only, amber accent
- Plan mode: blue accent, read-only exploration
- Follows Alive design language: quiet, restrained, no emojis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: permissionMode "forcedAccept" is invalid, canUseTool callback is dead code

The Claude Agent SDK CLI rejects "forcedAccept" — valid values are:
acceptEdits, bypassPermissions, default, delegate, dontAsk, plan.
Changed default stream mode to "default" and tightened the type.

More critically: empirical testing proves the SDK's canUseTool callback
is NEVER called (tested v0.2.41). The CLI auto-approves all tools
regardless of --permission-prompt-tool stdio. Our actual security
comes from allowedTools/disallowedTools arrays and cwd sandboxing.

Added loud warnings in worker-entry.mjs and CLAUDE.md rule #24.
Added verification script: scripts/verify-canUseTool-callback.mjs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gut dead canUseTool callback, add static check that bombs on violations

The SDK's canUseTool callback is never called (v0.2.41). Removed all
dead security code (path traversal, heavy bash blocking) from
worker-entry.mjs — it was providing zero protection.

Set canUseTool = undefined. Added scripts/validation/check-canUseTool-dead.sh
that explodes with bomb emojis if anyone adds deny logic, imports
createStreamCanUseTool, or re-enables the dead callback. Wired into
static-check so it runs on every pre-push and CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: sandboxed-fs MCP server, stream mode registry cleanup, dead code removal

- Add alive-sandboxed-fs MCP server with SDK-compatible tool names (Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash, NotebookEdit) for site workspace sandboxing
- Rename sdkTools → modeTools in StreamModeConfig to support both SDK and MCP tool names in mode filtering
- Add resolveStreamMode() for validated mode resolution with role fallback
- Harden E2E global teardown: filter managed users, guard org deletion with is_test_env, clean up workflow data
- Improve husky hooks: skip file checkouts, auto-install on lockfile change, early-exit pre-push when nothing new
- Remove dead code: workspace-service-manager.ts, install-package tests, unused shared exports
- Clean up agent-constants, executor, client-identifier, and worker-entry for refactored imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: superadmin site limit bypass, fix placeholder emails, clean deploy-dev

- Superadmins bypass site creation limits in deploy, deploy-subdomain, import-repo
- Replace team@example.com with team@alive.best in DeploymentStatus and integrations
- Remove shell-server-go rebuild from deploy-dev.sh (done separately via `make shell`)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use bare E2B template aliases instead of namespaced names

Self-hosted E2B now resolves bare aliases correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: E2B sandbox hardening — path security, dependency install, MCP isolation

- Use shared isPathWithinWorkspace instead of inlined boundary check in e2b-mcp
- Resolve relative paths against SANDBOX_WORKSPACE_ROOT in tool handlers
- Auto-install dependencies after file sync and on reconnect (node_modules excluded from sync)
- Strip non-e2b MCP servers in E2B mode to prevent leaking internal tools
- Allow NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED through worker env for self-hosted E2B

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: E2B sandbox routing — preview-proxy sandbox-map, preview URL injection

- Add sandbox-map.json alongside port-map.json for E2B domain routing
- Preview-proxy resolves sandbox backends via HTTPS to E2B host
- Bash tool appends preview URLs when dev servers start (no localhost)
- Consolidate domainToPreviewLabel/buildPreviewUrl into @webalive/shared
- Make dependency install non-throwing in sandbox manager

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

AVVIAMENTISTAMPA.EST1.1: spostato a reparto esterno (priorità 92)

Era erroneamente in stampa offset con priorità 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix NZ settlement, exotic anchoring, class signals, zombie recovery (#148) (#148)

* Fix tissue engine maiden race bias: career bands + market blend (#148)

The maiden_career band (0.004) was designed for 0-win horses in open races,
but in maiden races ALL horses have 0 wins. This caused a 220x cliff between
lightly-raced (1-2 starts) and exposed (3+) maidens, ranking $65 longshots
above $2.20 favourites.

Two fixes:
1. Maiden-specific career bands based on starts + place record (not wins)
2. Geometric mean blend (α=0.50) with market-implied probability in maiden
   races only, capturing trial form and trainer confidence signals

Validated on Kilmore R1: winner Druthers now ranks #1 (was #4), top-3
finishers in top-4 picks. Non-maiden races unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix generation timeout: add 30s timeout to learning context + fix NoneType crash

- Wrap _build_learning_context with asyncio.wait_for(timeout=30) so generation
  proceeds even if embedding/memory queries hang
- Fix NoneType crash in memory/store.py where track_condition=None caused
  .lower() to fail (use `or ""` instead of default param)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Betfair queue crash: stake_mode='auto' not handled in poll loop

Use get_current_stake() which already handles 'auto' mode via doubling
formula, instead of raw float() conversion that crashes every poll cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix session rollback after learning context timeout

When asyncio.wait_for cancels mid-query, the SQLAlchemy session is left
in an invalid transaction state. Add rollback() after timeout to recover
the session so content saving can proceed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix generation crash: isolate learning context in separate DB session

Root cause: learning context timeout cancelled mid-query, corrupting the
main async session. Even with rollback, the aiosqlite connection was
broken, causing MissingGreenlet when saving generated content.

Fix:
- _build_learning_context now runs in its own async_session() scope
- If it times out or crashes, the main generator session is untouched
- Cache meeting.venue in meets.py before async work to avoid lazy-load
  in error handler on broken session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Optimize learning context: cache memories, single-query stats

Performance fixes for _build_learning_context (was timing out at 30s):
- Cache race_memories query result for all runner lookups (was loading
  ALL 10K+ rows 30 times per meeting, now loads 500 most recent once)
- Merge get_stats() from 4 sequential queries into 1 using CASE/SUM
- Added composite indexes on server: (track,distance), (state,distance)
  on race_assessments and (settled_at,created_at) on race_memories

Expected time reduction: ~28-45s → ~5-8s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Save content before SSE yield + only post wraps for positive P&L meetings

Two fixes:
1. Move _save_content() before yield in generate_early_mail_stream so
   content persists even when SSE client disconnects during long AI calls
   (was losing generated content when browser timed out)

2. Wrap-up auto-posting now checks meeting P&L: only posts to Twitter/
   Facebook if the meeting had positive settled P&L. Negative meetings
   still get wrap-up generated and auto-approved, just not posted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix JSON block leaking into X long-form posts

_format_long_post bypassed strip_json_block, so the structured JSON
data block appended by the AI was included in the posted tweet text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add class movement and recent gelding signals to probability engine

Class movement: compares today's race class against last 1-3 starts from
form_history. Dropping in class (e.g. Open → BM64) boosts score up to +0.08;
stepping up penalises up to -0.06. Uses new _class_to_level() parser that
handles 386+ class string formats (BM, RTG, CL, Group, Maiden, etc).

Recent gelding: young geldings (≤4yo) with few career starts (≤10) get +0.06
boost recognising the "ultimate gear change" effect. Moderate boost (+0.03)
for ≤15 starts.

20 new tests covering class parser, class movement scenarios, and gelding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix exotic selection: remove blanket fav<$2 kill, enforce fav inclusion

The old guard killed ALL exotics when the favourite was under $2, but
RULE 1 already requires the favourite to be included in every exotic.
A Quinella 1,5 with a $1.98 fav is a valid play — the issue was
exotics that excluded the fav, not short-priced fav exotics.

Changes:
- Remove `return None` when fav_price < 2.0
- Add "Exotic: SKIP" message to pre-selections when no exotic recommended
  (prevents AI from inventing exotics from the combo table)
- Update prompt to respect SKIP instruction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tips link CTA below early mail title

Prompt now instructs AI to add "For all of Punty's tips for {venue},
head to https://punty.ai/tips/{meeting_id}" right after the title.
Meeting ID and tips link added to context data so the AI can construct it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add zombie bet recovery on scheduler startup

Bets stuck in 'placing' status from a crash/restart were never recovered.
On startup, re-queue bets whose race hasn't started, cancel those that
have already missed the window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix zombie recovery import path (punty.models.betfair_bet)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use PointsBet as primary results source for NZ venues instead of TAB Playwright

NZ venues (Te Aroha etc) were stuck on slow TabPlaywrightScraper (browser-based,
10-30s per request, frequent timeouts). Now uses 3-tier fast-first fallback:
1. PointsBet (HTTP API, ~200ms)
2. PuntingForm (HTTP API, ~300ms)
3. TAB Playwright (browser, last resort)

Applied to status checks, result scraping, and place dividend backfill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v3.3.0 — security hardening, rename to Outlook Assistant, CI improvements

Security:
- XSS prevention in OAuth callback with escapeHtml() (#60)
- CSRF protection with crypto.randomUUID() state parameter (#61)
- HTTP security headers on all auth server responses (#62)
- Error message sanitisation — truncated API errors, generic token errors (#63)
- Removed insecure /tmp token storage fallback (#64)

Added:
- CodeQL SAST scanning workflow (#65)
- Dependency review action blocks high-severity vulns in PRs (#66)
- Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged + commitlint (#67)

Changed:
- Renamed from "Outlook MCP" to "Outlook Assistant" across 34 files
- Bumped @modelcontextprotocol/sdk minimum from ^1.1.0 to ^1.27.1
- ESLint 10 + globals 17 + @eslint/js 10 major dependency bumps

Fixed:
- ESLint 10 compatibility (preserve-caught-error, no-useless-assignment)

Add README with CLI command reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(pipeline,llm): auto-detect LLM provider, self-correction, and hardening (#18)

* feat(pipeline,llm): auto-detect LLM provider, self-correction, and hardening

- LLM router auto-detects provider from model name (claude-* → anthropic,
  gemini-* → google_genai, gpt-* → openai) instead of hardcoding
  provider-model pairings. Fixes 404 when model names don't match providers.
- Add SQL self-correction: validation errors feed back to LLM up to
  max_correction_attempts before routing to human approval.
- Add extract_text() helper to normalize Gemini list content blocks to
  plain text across all pipeline agents.
- Block system catalog queries (sqlite_master, information_schema) in
  check_read_only() guard.
- Strengthen prompt to discourage schema exploration queries.
- Fix frontend API 404: align BASE URL with backend /api/v1 prefix.
- Fix approval dialog: buttons always visible with scrollable content.
- Update tests for self-correction flow and new validation guards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): strip inline # comments from model names in .env

pydantic-settings does not strip inline comments from .env values,
so `LIGHT_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6 # comment` was being sent as the
full model name to the API, causing 404 errors.

- Add field_validator to strip inline # comments from model name fields
- Fix .env.example to use separate comment lines
- Update secondary_model default from deprecated gemini-3-pro-preview
  to gemini-3.1-pro-preview
- Update default_model to claude-sonnet-4-6 (better price/performance)
- Set light_model default to gemini-2.5-flash

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fight mode: grok-4-1-fast-reasoning for deep analysis (unchanged)
- Fixes wrong event names/dates caused by Grok's lack of 2026 UFC schedule knowledge
- ESPN competitions reversed so main event is first
- Models: grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning (enrichment), grok-4-1-fast-reasoning (analysis)

commit.message:
ci: fix BMv2 build on Ubuntu — install system deps + multiarch path (#180)

The squash merge of #173 missed the CI workflow changes. BMv2's bmi
library needs pcap/pcap.h and libgmp at compile time. On Ubuntu 24.04,
libgmp-dev installs headers under the multiarch path
(/usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu), not /usr/include directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show Dauerbeleg on PDF stamps and Eigenbeleg documents

- Stamp: Shows "DAUERBELEG" label next to belegNumber when belegSubType is DAUERRECHNUNG
- Eigenbeleg PDF: Title becomes "DAUEREIGENBELEG" with subtitle "MANUELL ERFASSTER DAUERBELEG"
- Passes belegSubType through all stampPdf calls (approval, correction, rejection, export)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Google Sheets sync, NPA scoring, financial sector data fixes

- Add sync-sheet CLI command — reads symbols from col B, writes score
  to col C (numeric) and label+delta to col D (BUY (+7) etc.)
- Add screener/integrations/google_sheets.py with SheetSyncer
- Fix screener.in consolidated URL returning empty tables — _page_has_data
  now checks that the quarters table header has >= 2 columns
- Fix nan ROE/ROCE passing comparison checks; sanitize all ratio values
- Fix financial_sector config missing interest_coverage keys — merge with
  debt config so only overridden keys take effect
- Add bank/NBFC row label aliases (Interest Earned, Revenue from ops)
  so revenue/PAT/EPS populate for financial companies
- Add Gross NPA % and Net NPA % extraction, scoring (+10/-10 each),
  and dedicated Asset Quality table with 1Y/2Y/3Y pp changes
- Hide OCF/PAT Ratio row for financial sector companies
- Fix growth scoring to use worst of 3Y and 5Y avg (prevents 5Y masking
  recent deterioration like ESAFSFB PAT -70% 3Y)
- Fix valuation flag contradiction — P/E flag now matches scoring buckets
  (YELLOW when above 5Y mean, not a blanket GREEN)
- run.sh now routes scan/sync-sheet/clear-cache/version to correct command
- sync-sheet fails fast on any symbol error — no partial writes to sheet
- Add gspread and google-auth to requirements.txt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add E2E tests for webhook trigger on incoming mail (#878)

* test: add E2E tests for auto-reply trigger and webhook trigger

- Improve mock reply() in e2e_test_api.ts to send auto-replies via
  SMTP (WorkerMailer) so they reach Mailpit for verification
- Add auto-reply-trigger.spec.ts: verifies auto-reply is sent when
  incoming mail matches source_prefix, and NOT sent otherwise
- Add webhook-trigger.spec.ts: starts a temporary HTTP server to
  receive webhook calls, verifies payload on mail arrival

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fallback EmailMessage for E2E auto-reply when cloudflare:email is unavailable

In wrangler dev mode without Email Routing binding, `import('cloudflare:email')`
throws, silently caught by auto_reply's try-catch. Add a fallback that constructs
a plain object with a ReadableStream `raw` property so the E2E mock reply() can
send the auto-reply via SMTP to Mailpit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle both \r\n and \n line endings in MIME parser for E2E tests

mimetext uses os.EOL which is \n on Linux (Docker). The parseMimeForReply
function only looked for \r\n, causing it to fail parsing the auto-reply
MIME content in the E2E environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add debug logging and robust raw MIME extraction for E2E auto-reply

- auto_reply.ts: add fallback ReadableStream when cloudflare:email
  is unavailable, attach rawMime directly to replyMessage
- e2e_test_api.ts: try reading rawMime string first, then fallback
  to ReadableStream; add diagnostic console.log for CI debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip sealed EmailMessage in E2E mode, await webhook server listen

- auto_reply.ts: use plain object with raw ReadableStream in E2E_TEST_MODE
  (cloudflare:email's EmailMessage is sealed, can't attach extra properties)
- e2e_test_api.ts: simplify mock reply() to read raw ReadableStream directly,
  add defensive check for from without @
- webhook-trigger.spec.ts: await server.listen to ensure socket is bound
  before sending requests (CodeRabbit review feedback)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing await for async startWebhookReceiver in disabled test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: drop auto-reply E2E test, clean up webhook test

- Remove auto-reply-trigger.spec.ts (cannot test without modifying
  production auto_reply.ts due to sealed EmailMessage from cloudflare:email)
- Clean up e2e_test_api.ts: remove WorkerMailer, MIME parsing, and SMTP
  reply logic that was only needed for auto-reply testing
- Improve webhook test: use dynamic port allocation (port 0) instead of
  hardcoded WEBHOOK_PORT to avoid port conflicts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: assert webhook request path in E2E test

Add path assertion to verify webhook request hits /webhook endpoint,
preventing false positives from incorrect routing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Facebook Marketplace integration (Phase 1)

BACKEND:
- Create marketplace_settings table (credentials, sync config)
- Create marketplace_listings table (tracks listing status per vehicle)
- Create marketplace_sync_log table (audit log)
- Add sync-to-facebook edge function (transforms inventory, calls Graph API)
- Rate limiting: 100ms between requests
- Auto-updates marketplace_listings with sync status

FRONTEND:
- Create MarketplaceSettingsPage (OAuth, sync controls, stats)
- Create MarketplaceStatusBadge component (shows FB/KSL/AT status per vehicle)
- Update InventoryPage: add marketplace status badges
- Add /marketplaces route and navigation

FEATURES:
- Manual sync button (sync all inventory to Facebook)
- Auto-sync toggle (hourly/daily/realtime)
- Sync status tracking (last sync, success/error count)
- Detailed error messages
- Facebook OAuth flow (ready for credentials)

NEXT STEPS:
- User needs to configure Facebook App ID in env vars
- User needs to run migration: supabase db push --include-all
- Connect Facebook Business account via OAuth
- Test sync with real credentials

commit.message:
Upgrade all @nonoun packages to latest

- native-ui: 0.7.34 → 0.7.39
  CSS: n-body gap doubled (2→4), n-card gap zeroed, n-header padding
  aligned, sub-container padding via custom properties, button/input
  max-height. Lifecycle hardening for ready promise and DragController.

- native-app: 0.3.15 → 0.3.17
  Semantic sub-containers (header/main/footer) in n-app-panel.
  Sidebar nav teardown null safety.

- native-chat: 0.5.21 → 0.5.24
  Chat starter CSS preset, session bootstrap, A2UI session layer
  alignment. Breaking changes from 0.5.18 (data-role, gateway) were
  already migrated.

- native-a2ui: 0.1.15 → 0.2.3 (MAJOR)
  Session manager, surface registry, adapters (A2A, AG-UI, direct),
  catalog builder. Host APIs (createA2UIAdapter, receive, workbench
  element) are backward compatible.

- native-chart: 0.1.5 → 0.1.7
- native-tokens: 0.5.13 → 0.5.15
- native-codemirror: 0.2.18 → 0.2.20
- native-editor: 0.2.13 → 0.2.15
- native-playground: 0.2.13 → 0.2.15

No host-side migration needed. Build passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
